'''Zachary Cini''' (born 24 May 2000) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Cini was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales|Sydney]] and is of Maltese descent.

Cini played his junior rugby league for the Minchinbury Jets. He later said, "I grew up supporting the Tigers. I loved [[Benji Marshall|Benji]] in 2005 and I used to sit in the lounge room watching him on TV. When he played the grand final I was five and I used to go out into the yard and try to practise the Benji step."<ref name=debut>{{cite web| work= nrl.com| title=A tale of two Zacs: Cini stars in Tigers upset as Lomax injured|author=Brad Walter | date=2 May 2021| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/02/lomax-suffers-suspected-broken-thumb-in-tigers-tussle/}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Cini came through the [[Penrith Panthers]] junior system playing in their [[SG Ball Cup|SG Ball]] championship winning team of 2018, he was educated at [[St Dominic's College, Penrith]] where he made the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team]] in that same year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://westernweekender.com.au/2018/08/zac-follows-in-famous-footsteps/ |title=Zac follows in famous footsteps |publisher=The Western Weekender |date=3 August 2018}}</ref>

===2021===
Cini was named to his debut in round 8 for the Tigers, starting on the wing against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] away at [[WIN Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/04/27/nrl-round-8-squad-announcements/ |title=Round 8 Team Lists |publisher=NRL.com |date=1 May 2021}}</ref> At the time he was leading the lower [[NSW Cup]] competition in post-contact metres, tackle breaks, runs and run metres.<ref>{{cite web| work= nrl.com | title=Stats | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210502050611/https://www.nrl.com/stats/?competition=113&season=2021|archivedate=2 May 2021| url=https://www.nrl.com/stats/?competition=113&season=2021}}</ref> He scored a try and had another disallowed as he "starred" in his first game.<ref name=debut/> He said after the match, "I got about 20 tickets for family and my mates organised a bus. I think there was about 50 of them that jumped on the bus and sat in that corner. You could definitely hear them. It is a dream just to play footy but to score on debut with my mates in that corner, just the support they gave was awesome and made it 10 times better."<ref name=debut/> [[The Sydney Morning Herald]] said, "The flying mullet man from Minchinbury, a mad Wests Tigers fan, became an instant cult hero."<ref>{{cite news| work= Sydney Morning Herald | title=Flying mullet man from Minchinbury: Cini an instant cult hero | url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/mullet-man-cini-an-instant-cult-hero-the-story-behind-tigers-rookie-20210502-p57o7h.html}}</ref>

Cini played a total of four matches for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 13th and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-fans-savage-the-clubs-newlook-logo/news-story/83f356c5f7c0d336d950d750f4e054b5/|title=Wests Tigers fans savage the club's new-look logo|newspaper=News.com.au|date=6 October 2021|last1=Conrad|first1=Alex}}</ref> In December 2021, Cini was released by the Wests Tigers and he joined [[NSW Cup]] side [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newtownjets.com/christmas-and-new-year-greetings-from-the-newtown-jets/|title=Christmas and New Year Greetings from the Newtown Jets|website=www.newtownjets.com}}{{Dead link|date=February 2026 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref>

===2022===
Before the start of the 2022 NSW Cup season, Cini departed Newtown and signed a contract to join [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]'s NSW Cup team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2022/05/29/raiders-nsw-cup-defeated-in-two-point-thriller-against-eels/|title=Raiders NSW Cup Defeated In Two-point Thriller Against Eels|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
Cini made no appearances for Parramatta's first grade team in 2023. He would instead play for the clubs NSW Cup side making 20 appearances and scoring eleven tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-news-2024-best-reserve-grade-players-nsw-cup-queensland-cup-dean-hawkins-kayal-iro-deine-mariner-tom-jenkins/news-story/1207201685350d560e962ab25719ec87/|title=Souths half to force awkward call; 102kg Eels beast ready to erupt — 15 ressies set to break into NRL|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
On 21 August, it was announced that Cini would join [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal from 2025.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 August 2024 |title=Castleford sign Parramatta Eels back Cini |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cdey34y5d66o |access-date=22 August 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=21 August 2024 |title=Zac Cini aiming high with Castleford Tigers after joining from Paramatta Eels |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/zac-cini-aiming-high-with-castleford-tigers-after-joining-from-paramatta-eels-4751833 |access-date=22 August 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> Cini made no first-grade appearances for Parramatta in 2024, instead featuring for the club's NSW Cup team.<ref>{{cite web |date=11 September 2024 |title=Eels farewell departing players |url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2024/09/11/eels-farewell-departing-players/ |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

===2025===
Cini was assigned squad number 3 for Castleford and made his debut on 9 February against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[2025 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=6 December 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2025 squad numbers as new-look spine formed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2025-squad-numbers-as-new-look-spine-formed |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=9 February 2025 |title=Bradford upset Cas as Salford & Giants go through |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce8jm7y7m34o |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 1 of the Super League campaign, Cini scored a try in the Tigers' 19–18 golden point extra-time defeat against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cresswell |first=Matthew |date=14 February 2025 |title=Hull KR claim golden-point victory over Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/clyk713p07mo |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Having began the season playing as left centre, Cini moved onto the right edge from round 4 in a swap with [[Sam Wood (rugby league, born 1997)|Sam Wood]].<ref name="RLRKC" /> He scored one try against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in round 3, [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in round 7, and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in round 11.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lanigan |first=Adam |date=2 March 2025 |title=Leeds cruise to victory over struggling Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4ymrleyjeo |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=12 April 2025 |title=Leigh put losses behind them to win at Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4g43jve342o |access-date=14 April 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=18 May 2025 |title=Hat-trick for Hoy as Cas thump struggling Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4ge3ewpdq7o |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Following a standout performance filling in at fullback in round 14, head coach [[Danny McGuire]] singled Cini out for praise, stating that he had "been really good for us all year."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=14 June 2025 |title=Danny McGuire names 'under the radar' Castleford star as unused sub explained |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-mcguire-names-under-the-radar-castleford-star-as-unused-sub-explained |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

Cini was sidelined for six weeks by a facial fracture suffered against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in August.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=6 August 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers rocked by serious injury as Chris Chester seeks major improvement against Hull KR |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-rocked-by-serious-injury-as-chris-chester-eyeing-major-improvement-against-hull-kr/ |access-date=28 December 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2 August 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers star’s horrendous "Rocky Balboa" injury revealed following St Helens loss |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-stars-horrendous-rocky-balboa-injury-revealed-following-st-helens-loss/ |url-status=live |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20250918030405/https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-stars-horrendous-rocky-balboa-injury-revealed-following-st-helens-loss/ |archive-date=18 September 2025 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> He made 22 appearances throughout the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]] as the club finished 11th on the table.

=== 2026 ===
Cini was ruled out of the opening rounds of the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]] by a hamstring injury sustained during pre-season training.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jackson |first=Will |date=23 January 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers injury latest amid setbacks for Zac Cini and overseas recruit |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-injury-latest-amid-setbacks-for-zac-cini-and-overseas-recruit-112018/ |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> For six matches following his return to the side in round 3, Cini deputised at fullback due to injuries to [[Blake Taaffe]] and [[Fletcher Rooney]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=5 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers rocked by fresh injuries as Fletcher Rooney faces surgery |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-rocked-fresh-injuries-1719771 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> With Castleford in the market for a fullback replacement,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=21 April 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers star offered to clubs for immediate transfer |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-star-offered-to-clubs-for-immediate-transfer |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> it was speculated that Cini could transfer to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in April,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=22 April 2026 |title=Bradford Bulls hit transfer snag in pursuit of Castleford Tigers star |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-hit-transfer-snag-in-pursuit-of-castleford-tigers-star |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=23 April 2026 |title=Ryan Carr addresses Zac Cini future at Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ryan-carr-addresses-zac-cini-future-at-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> though in round 10 he returned to the team in his primary position of right centre.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=1 May 2026 |title=Error-ridden Castleford Tigers are no match for in-form champions Hull KR |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/error-ridden-castleford-tigers-are-no-match-for-in-form-champions-hull-kr-8370868 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref>
